hero.title: Lord Sri Balaji Mandir
hero.subtitle: & Other Sacred Information
hero.description: A humble attempt to share information about Sri Swami Venkatesha — the Kali Yuga avatar of Lord Vishnu residing on the Saptagiri mountain range at Tirumala, Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh.

about.title: About This Blog
about.subtitle: An Open Book
about.text: From childhood, we have heard about gods and their appearances from our elders, and seen various gods and their miracles through movies and TV. We all have a deep desire to see, know, and experience God personally. All these experiences can be felt at Tirumala — the sacred place on the Saptagiri mountain range in Tirupati district (old district Guntur), Andhra Pradesh. It is the fortune of all earth's residents that we can witness Lord Vishnu in person at this most sacred and spiritually powerful place.

history.title: Mythological History
history.content: Lord Venkateswara, also known as Balaji, Srinivasa, and Govinda, is a form of Lord Vishnu who is said to have taken abode at Tirumala to liberate all beings of the Kali Yuga. Ancient scriptures describe this sacred mountain as one of the most powerful spiritual centers on Earth. The seven peaks of Tirumala represent the seven heads of Adishesha — the cosmic serpent on whom Lord Vishnu rests.

list.title: The Sacred Flag
list.desc: The flag atop the temple always faces the wind, regardless of its direction — a divine phenomenon observed daily.

list.title: The Eternal Lamp
list.desc: The sacred lamp in the sanctum has been burning continuously for hundreds of years without interruption.

list.title: The Fragrant Deity
list.desc: The idol of Lord Venkateswara emanates a natural fragrance without any external perfume.

list.title: The Divine Hair
list.desc: The hair on the idol remains soft and tangle-free, and devotees believe it grows naturally.

list.title: The Chakra Miracle
list.desc: The Sudarshana Chakra (disc) on the temple tower can be seen from all four sides simultaneously.

list.title: The Temperature Mystery
list.desc: The backside of the idol feels cool in summer and warm in winter — a mystery that defies natural explanation.

places.name: Sri Venkateswara Temple
places.desc: The main temple on Tirumala hill, one of the most visited pilgrimage sites in the world.

places.name: Akasaganga
places.desc: A sacred waterfall where devotees bathe before darshan.

places.name: Papavinasam
places.desc: A waterfall believed to cleanse all sins of devotees who bathe here.

places.name: Sri Padmavathi Temple
places.desc: Located in Tiruchanur, dedicated to Goddess Padmavathi, consort of Lord Venkateswara.

places.name: Kapila Theertham
places.desc: A sacred waterfall and Shiva temple at the base of Tirumala hills.

places.name: Srivari Museum
places.desc: A museum displaying the history and artifacts of the Tirumala temple.

steps.title: Book Online
steps.desc: Visit TTD's official website to book darshan tickets, accommodation, and hair tonsure slots in advance.

steps.title: Travel to Tirupati
steps.desc: Tirupati is well connected by air, rail, and road. The nearest airport is Tirupati Airport.

steps.title: Climb or Take Bus
steps.desc: You can either walk (about 12 km) via the traditional path or take TTD buses from Tirupati.

steps.title: Queue & Darshan
steps.desc: Join the queue at the temple complex. Darshan types include free, special entry (Rs. 300), and VIP.

steps.title: Laddu Prasadam
steps.desc: After darshan, collect the famous Tirupati Laddu — sanctioned prasadam available to all devotees.

precautions.title: Precautions
precautions.subtitle: Important Guidelines for Devotees
list: Wear traditional attire — dhoti/sari or formal clothes. Western clothes like shorts and sleeveless tops are not permitted.
Do not carry mobile phones, cameras, or electronic devices inside the temple.
Maintain absolute silence and discipline inside the sanctum sanctorum.
Remove footwear before entering the temple premises.
Avoid eating non-vegetarian food during the pilgrimage.
Keep your token/ticket safe — it is required for entry.
Stay hydrated, especially during summer months when queues can be long.
Elderly and disabled devotees have special facilities — inquire at TTD counters.

prasadam.title: Annaprasadam
prasadam.subtitle: Sacred Community Meal
prasadam.text: The TTD (Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams) provides free meals to lakhs of devotees daily at the Annaprasadam complex. This sacred community meal consists of rice, sambar, rasam, and other traditional dishes. It is served without any discrimination — rich or poor, all eat together as equals before the Lord. The meal is considered a blessing from the deity himself.

info.label: Accommodation
info.value: Available at various TTD guest houses

mundan.title: Mundan (Hair Sacrifice)
mundan.subtitle: Why and Where to Perform
mundan.description: Devotees offer their hair as a symbol of sacrificing something dear to God. It is a widespread tradition in South India for both children and adults.
locations: Main Kalyankatta (Near temple entrance, next to shopping complex)
process: Biometric (eye scan) registration is required first.
A token and a fresh blade are provided (indicates hall and barber number).
Head must be wetted before starting to avoid injury.
The process typically takes 1 to 1.5 hours.
To offer only 3 strands, inform the staff as 'Modu Katteerlu'.

tripPlanning.title: Trip Planning
tripPlanning.subtitle: Pune to Tirupati - 5 Day Itinerary

itinerary.event: Pune to Renigunta by Train (Departure 4:15 PM)
Arrival 8:00 AM at Renigunta, Freshen up at Srinivasam Complex, Local Temple Tour (Kapila Teertham, ISKCON, Padmavati Temple)
Travel to Tirumala, Mundan, and Main Darshan (Time slot around 8:00 PM - 10:00 PM)
Temple circumambulation, Bedi Anjaneya Swamy Darshan, Check out and return journey

tripPlanning.budgetNote: Note: Food cost is minimal due to the Free Annaprasadalaya service.

footer.blessings: May Lord Venkateswara bless you with divine grace and prosperity
footer.description: An informational platform dedicated to sharing knowledge about Sri Venkateswara Temple (Tirupati Balaji) - the abode of Lord Vishnu through personal experiences and verified information.
